title: Add scheduled posts with timezone-aware delivery and admin reschedule
business_context: Marketing wants to schedule posts in advance with timezone-aware delivery. Currently posts publish immediately. Need scheduling, reliable delivery, and reschedule UI.
constraints: Times stored in UTC but displayed in user timezone
reasoning: Schedule field on Post. Worker every 30s scans for scheduled posts due. Atomic claim via findOneAndUpdate with status=scheduled and scheduledFor<=now. Reschedule until 5min before via conditional update. UI shows in user timezone using Intl.

after_state.description: Scheduled posts + cron publisher + admin reschedule.

files.purpose: Post with schedule status
Create with optional schedule and reschedule guard
Worker scans due posts and publishes atomically
Input in user timezone; converts to UTC ISO on change

migration_strategy: Step 3: Validate by scheduling test posts
testing_strategy: Schedule test: schedule 1 min in future, assert published. Reschedule: only allowed >5 min out. Concurrent claim: two workers, only one publishes.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would skip the atomic claim - two workers double-publish.

title: Add live progress bar for long-running jobs via Server-Sent Events
business_context: Job processing reports progress to console. Users have no visibility. Need live progress streamed to UI.
constraints: Multiple users watching same job ok

files.content: export async function processImport(jobId: string) { /* runs */ }

reasoning: Worker updates JobProgress { jobId, percent, message } in Redis pub/sub. SSE endpoint subscribes to channel for job. Auto-closes on done. Reconnect re-subscribes; latest snapshot stored in Redis hash.

after_state.description: Pub/sub + SSE progress endpoint with snapshot.

files.purpose: Sets latest snapshot and publishes progress
SSE bridge to Redis pub/sub; sends snapshot first, then live updates
SSE-driven progress bar with auto-close on done

testing_strategy: Reconnect: drop SSE, reconnect, assert snapshot delivered. Done auto-close: status=done, assert client closes. Multi-watch: two browsers watch same job, both update.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would poll for status - misses intermediate updates. SSE pushes immediately.

title: Replace Express custom router with typed route registration via decorators-free helper
business_context: Routes registered via Express boilerplate in many files. Want centralized registration with types tying route to handler.
constraints: Type safety on params and body

issues: No type ties between schema and handler

reasoning: defineRoute helper takes { method, path, schema?, handler }. Types ensure handler signature matches schema. Aggregated and registered on Express in one place.

after_state.description: Typed route registration with schema-derived handler types.

files.purpose: Helper that ties Zod schemas to typed handler args
Routes declared declaratively with type-tied schemas

migration_strategy: Step 2: Migrate one route file at a time
Step 3: Keep old routes during transition
testing_strategy: Schema validation: invalid params returns 400. Handler types: tsc errors on misuse. Backward compat: existing middleware still applies.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would use decorators - requires reflect-metadata and complicates tsc. Helper-based approach is type-safe and dependency-free.

title: Add 3D camera tour with predefined waypoints triggered from UI
business_context: Marketing wants a guided tour of a 3D scene with annotated waypoints. Users click 'Next' to fly to next waypoint with smooth camera animation.
reasoning: Camera controller component animates position + target via lerp over duration. Waypoints array. Next/Prev buttons drive currentIndex.

after_state.description: Waypoint-driven camera tour with Next/Prev and pause.

files.purpose: Animates camera between waypoints with smoothstep easing
UI with prev/next; smoothstep camera animation

testing_strategy: Next: camera animates to waypoint. Smoothness: assert FPS stable. Bounds: prev at index 0 stays at 0.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would teleport camera position - jarring. Lerp + smoothstep is the right easing.

title: Add tenant-aware webhook event filtering with subscription topics
business_context: Currently all webhook subscribers receive all org events. Need topic-based subscriptions so tenants pick which events to receive.
constraints: Backward compat - existing subscribers default to all
reasoning: Add topics array of glob patterns. Matcher checks event type against patterns. Default '*' for legacy. Validation on patterns.

after_state.description: Topic patterns + matcher + UI to manage.

files.purpose: Subscriptions with topic patterns
Glob matcher with cache
Fire filters by topic before enqueue

migration_strategy: Step 1: Add topics field defaulting to ['*']
Step 2: Build admin UI for picking topics
testing_strategy: Glob test: 'orders.*' matches 'orders.created' but not 'invoice.created'. Default test: legacy subs get all events. Per-tenant: subscription on tenant A doesn't receive tenant B events.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would compare strings exactly - no wildcard support. Glob matching with regex cache is correct.

title: Add session fixation prevention by rotating session id on privilege change
business_context: Session id stays the same after login. An attacker who got the pre-login id can hijack the post-login session. Need session id rotation on login + role change.

files.content: export const login = async (req: any, res: any) => { req.session.userId = 'u1'; res.json({ ok: true }); };

reasoning: req.session.regenerate() before assigning user id - creates fresh id. Same on impersonation stop and role escalation. Audit rotation.

after_state.description: Session regeneration on privilege change + audit.

files.purpose: Regenerates session id on login and impersonation stop

migration_strategy: Step 2: Update flows that change effective user
Step 3: Test session-cookie value changes after login
testing_strategy: Cookie test: pre-login Cookie, login, assert new sid. Impersonation: stop assigns real user id with new sid.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would only set userId without regenerating - attacker who knew pre-login sid still has it.

title: Stream large query results via async iterators instead of buffering
business_context: Reports endpoint loads 200k rows into memory before serializing. OOM under load. Need cursor-based streaming JSON.
reasoning: Manually stream JSON array using cursor. Write '[' then comma-separated JSON objects then ']'. Errors mid-stream send 'error' as separate event or simply close - document behavior.

after_state.description: Manual streaming JSON via cursor.

files.purpose: Cursor streaming JSON; commas added between rows

migration_strategy: Step 3: Document behavior of mid-stream errors
testing_strategy: Memory test: 200k rows, RSS stays bounded. Format test: response parses as JSON array.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would use JSON.stringify on the lean array - same memory problem.

title: Replace stale-checking polling with focus-revalidation + SWR cache
business_context: Dashboard widgets poll every 30s to stay fresh. Tab in background still polls, wasting battery/bandwidth. Switch to revalidate-on-focus.
constraints: Cache shared across widgets reading same endpoint
reasoning: Use useResource (built earlier in fs-115) which already supports focus revalidation. Switch widgets to it.

after_state.description: SWR-style cache + focus revalidation.

files.purpose: Resource hook handles focus and online revalidation

migration_strategy: Step 3: Measure battery drain in mobile lab
testing_strategy: Background test: blur, assert no network call. Focus: assert refetch. Shared cache: two widgets, one fetch.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would gate setInterval on document.hidden - works but still polls when focused. Focus revalidation is sufficient.

title: Add point cloud renderer for LIDAR scans with chunked streaming and LOD
business_context: Surveying app receives LIDAR scans up to 50M points. Need efficient point cloud rendering with LOD downsampling and streaming chunks.

files.content:
export function LidarViewer() { return
Coming soon
; }

reasoning: Server splits cloud into octree chunks. Client requests chunks visible in camera frustum. Each chunk has multiple LOD levels. Use Points with BufferGeometry; one geometry per visible chunk. Streaming via fetch streams + parse binary format.

after_state.description: Chunked LIDAR viewer with LOD and frustum culling.

files.purpose: Chunk encapsulates BufferGeometry + Points and disposal
Viewer with frustum culling, LOD selection, and dispose discipline

migration_strategy: Step 1: Backend cuts cloud into octree chunks with LOD
testing_strategy: Big-cloud test: 50M points, assert 30+fps. LOD: move camera, assert chunk LOD swaps. Dispose: navigate away, assert geometries freed.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would load entire cloud into one BufferGeometry - OOM and no LOD. Chunked with frustum culling + LOD is the standard pattern.

title: Add live game lobby with countdown timer synchronized across clients
business_context: Game lobby starts countdown when 2+ players join. All players' clocks must show same remaining time despite latency.

files.content:
export function Lobby() { return
Waiting...
; }

reasoning: Server stores lobby.startsAt timestamp. Broadcasts startsAt. Clients compute remaining=startsAt - serverTime (using a small clock sync offset). Server can pause by setting startsAt=null and re-broadcasting.

after_state.description: Server-authoritative startsAt + client clock offset + live countdown.

files.purpose: Server-authoritative lobby with startsAt and broadcast
Client computes remaining using clock offset

migration_strategy: Step 3: Validate sync via testing two browsers
testing_strategy: Sync test: two browsers' countdowns within 200ms of each other. Drop test: one disconnects, countdown pauses.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would broadcast remaining time periodically - drift between broadcasts. Sending startsAt + serverTime once per update is correct.

title: Add CSV upload with column mapping UI before import
business_context: Users import CSVs with arbitrary column names. Currently fails when columns differ from expected. Need mapping UI: user matches their columns to our fields.
reasoning: Step 1: client uploads CSV, server parses header + first 5 rows, returns columns. Step 2: client posts mapping {sourceColumn: targetField}. Step 3: server runs import using mapping. Validation per row using Zod.

after_state.description: Two-step import with preview and mapping.

files.purpose: Preview endpoint returns headers + sample; import runs with mapping
UI for preview + mapping + run

migration_strategy: Step 3: Support large CSVs via streaming (per fs-077) later
testing_strategy: Mapping test: pick mapping, assert correct fields. Required missing: error. Invalid row: error captured but other rows imported.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would skip preview and force exact column names - usability fail.

title: Move from session-cookie auth to token+refresh for SPA but keep cookies for legacy server-rendered pages
business_context: Adding mobile app and SPA needing token auth. But existing legacy pages still use session cookies. Need both flows coexisting cleanly.
constraints: No flow regression for legacy users
Issuance scoped by user agent / endpoint

files.content: export const login = async (req: any, res: any) => { req.session.userId = 'u1'; res.json({ ok: true }); };

reasoning: Login returns either { token, refresh } or sets session cookie based on Accept header / endpoint variant. Two endpoints: /auth/login (cookie) and /auth/login/token (tokens). Both share underlying service.

after_state.description: Two endpoints sharing service; SPA uses tokens, legacy uses cookies.

files.purpose: Shared credential verification
Two endpoints share verifyCredentials

testing_strategy: Both flows: cookie login, token login; assert respective outputs. Cross-flow: legacy session continues independent of token endpoint use.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would convert legacy pages to tokens in one go - breaks SSR access.

title: Add query result caching with cache keys derived from query + tenant
business_context: Read-heavy endpoint hits Mongo aggregations repeatedly. Need cache with deterministic keys including tenant and query parameters.
reasoning: Redis-backed cache with TTL. Key includes orgId + canonical JSON of params. On Order write, invalidate by pattern. LRU is not needed since Redis with TTL handles eviction.

after_state.description: Redis cache with tenant-scoped keys + write-side invalidation.

files.purpose: Cache-aside with tenant-scoped scan invalidation
Post-save hook invalidates report cache for affected org

testing_strategy: Cache hit: second request returns same. Invalidation: write order, assert cache cleared. Isolation: org A and B have separate keys.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would use TTL only without invalidation - stale data after writes. Or namespace keys without tenant - cross-tenant leaks via cache.

title: Add per-route input size limits and timeout to prevent slowloris and ReDoS
business_context: Some endpoints hang for minutes on huge inputs. Slowloris-style clients keep connections open. Need per-route size + time budget.
reasoning: Per-route middleware: req.setTimeout + body size limit via express.json({limit}). For long-running endpoints (SSE), explicitly opt out.

after_state.description: Per-route timeouts + body size limits with SSE opt-out.

files.purpose: Per-route limits set timeout + monitor body size
Routes declare their tolerance for size and time

migration_strategy: Step 2: Apply to risk endpoints first
Step 3: Tune per route based on telemetry
testing_strategy: Slow client test: drip body bytes, assert connection killed after timeout. Body too large: assert 413. SSE: no impact.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would set a single global timeout - kills legitimate long-running endpoints. Per-route is the correct granularity.

title: Refactor wizard form state from prop-drilling to FSM with persistence
business_context: Multi-step wizard has 12 steps, state drilled through every component. Going back loses data. Need a state machine + persistence so users can resume.
reasoning: useWizard hook returns FSM state + transitions. Reducer keyed by step. Persists via localStorage. Each step component takes only its slice.

after_state.description: FSM-like wizard hook with persistence and per-step validation.

files.purpose: FSM hook with persistence and step-validation gating
Wizard uses hook; no prop drilling

migration_strategy: Step 3: Add resume UX surfacing saved state
testing_strategy: Persistence: refresh page mid-wizard, state restored. Validation gating: invalid blocks next. Reset clears state.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would adopt xstate for a 2-step wizard - overkill. A 60-line reducer suffices.

title: Add HTML email template engine with localization and preview
business_context: Emails sent via string concat. Need template engine with localization, partials, and admin preview UI.

files.content: export async function sendEmail({ to, subject, body }: { to: string; subject: string; body: string }) { /* SMTP */ }

reasoning: MJML templates per locale with handlebars partials. Compile at boot, cache compiled functions. Preview endpoint renders to HTML for admin review.

after_state.description: Compiled MJML+handlebars templates with locale + preview.

files.purpose: Compile MJML+Handlebars once; render with locale fallback
Service uses compiled templates
Admin preview endpoint

migration_strategy: Step 2: Migrate one email type at a time
testing_strategy: Render test: known template+vars produces expected HTML. Locale fallback: missing locale uses en. Injection: handlebars escapes by default.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would concat HTML strings - XSS risk via unescaped variables.

title: Add post-processing bloom effect for emissive scenes with selective mesh masking
business_context: Hero scene has glowing elements that should bloom. Adding bloom to entire scene washes out non-emissive parts. Need selective bloom.
constraints: Reduced motion disables bloom for performance

files.content:
import { Canvas } from '@react-three/fiber';
export function Scene() { return <Canvas><mesh><sphereGeometry /><meshStandardMaterial emissive="#ff0" emissiveIntensity={2} /></mesh></Canvas>; }

reasoning: Use selective bloom via two-pass render: render scene normally to one target, render only emissive meshes (others to black material) to another, blur + composite. Use @react-three/postprocessing for ergonomic setup.

after_state.description: Selective bloom via postprocessing layers.

files.purpose: Selective bloom isolated to sun mesh; reduced motion disables

testing_strategy: Visual: only tagged mesh glows. Reduced motion: no bloom. FPS test: assert >55fps.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would add full-scene Bloom - washes out diffuse meshes.

title: Add tenant-scoped analytics dashboard with read-only role
business_context: Tenants want analytics on their own data; ops also want analytics for support. Need read-only analytics role and per-tenant scope.
constraints: Ops with cross-tenant role bypasses tenant filter with audit

files.content: export function requireRole(role: string) { return (req: any, _res: any, next: any) => { if (req.user?.role !== role) return next(new Error('FORBIDDEN')); next(); }; }

reasoning: Roles: user, admin, analyst, support. analyst is read-only within own tenant. support is read-only cross-tenant with audit. Middleware checks role and route allowance.

after_state.description: Role-aware middleware + analyst/support roles + cross-tenant audit.

files.purpose: Role enforcement + read-only guard + cross-tenant audit
Analytics routes use role middleware chain

testing_strategy: Analyst test: GET allowed, POST blocked. Support cross-tenant: audit fires. Admin: full access.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would do role check inline per route - drift. Centralized middleware chain enforces consistently.

title: Add live order tracking page with map and ETA updates
business_context: Delivery tracking page shows static order. Need live driver position + ETA updates as driver moves.

files.content:
export function OrderTracker() { return
Static map placeholder
; }

reasoning: Driver app pushes coords to server. Server computes ETA via routing service every 5s and broadcasts to order room. Customer subscribes; minimal info exposed (no driver pii). Position smoothed via lerp on client.

after_state.description: Driver position + ETA streaming + map with smoothed marker.

files.purpose: Customer can track only their own order; redacted updates
Tracker subscribes to own order's room

testing_strategy: Auth test: try to track another user's order, blocked. Update test: server emits, customer sees. PII test: payload contains no driver name.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would include driver's identity - privacy fail.

title: Reduce TLS handshake cost via session resumption and HTTP keep-alive tuning
business_context: High RPS endpoint has high TLS handshake overhead. Need TLS session tickets + keep-alive tuning + connection pooling.
reasoning: Set TLS session timeout high, enable session tickets. Set HTTP keep-alive timeout. Outbound HTTP uses an agent with keepAlive=true and bounded sockets.

after_state.description: TLS tickets + keep-alive + outbound agent.

files.purpose: TLS session tickets + keep-alive + outbound pool

migration_strategy: Step 3: Pass agent into HTTP clients
testing_strategy: TLS test: tcpdump shows session resumption on subsequent handshakes. Latency: TTFB drops noticeably.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would only tune keep-alive but not enable session tickets - misses major win.

title: Carve out billing into its own module with adapter interface
business_context: Billing logic spread across services. Replacing Stripe with multi-PSP support is impossible. Need adapter interface and module isolation.
reasoning: Define BillingProvider interface. StripeProvider implements. Module exports a registry; consumers depend on interface not provider.

after_state.description: BillingProvider interface + Stripe implementation + registry.

files.purpose: Interface decouples from Stripe specifics
Stripe-backed implementation
Lazy singleton; swappable in tests
Consumer uses interface; no Stripe import

migration_strategy: Step 4: Add PayPal/Adyen later as new providers
testing_strategy: Interface test: mock provider, service works. Stripe test: integration test with Stripe test keys.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would keep Stripe imports scattered - migrating means searching every file.

title: Add device fingerprint binding for refresh tokens to prevent token theft replay
business_context: Refresh tokens are valid from any device. Stolen refresh used elsewhere works. Bind refresh to device fingerprint.
constraints: Legitimate device changes (browser update) shouldn't lock out

files.content: // see fs-044

reasoning: On refresh issuance, store hash of device fingerprint (UA + lang + tz). On refresh, compare; mismatch requires re-auth. Allow partial match using fuzzy similarity to handle UA changes.

after_state.description: Device-bound refresh with fuzzy match.

files.purpose: Hash-based fingerprint + char-prefix similarity score
Refresh binds fingerprint and rejects strong mismatches

migration_strategy: Step 1: Add fingerprint to refresh issuance
Step 2: Soft-enforce in log-only mode for one week
Step 4: Document re-auth on device change
testing_strategy: Stolen token test: present refresh from different UA, blocked. Browser update: minor UA change, similarity > 0.85, allowed.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would require exact match - too brittle, locks out users on minor UA changes.

title: Replace one-component-per-route lazy with route data loaders
business_context: Routes lazy-load components but data loads inside component effect - extra waterfall. Need React Router data loaders.
reasoning: Define loaders that return data; component uses useLoaderData. Loader runs in parallel with chunk download.

after_state.description: Route loaders with useLoaderData.

files.purpose: Loader + component uses useLoaderData
Router definition with loaders and errorElement

testing_strategy: Parallel test: navigation latency drops because data loads alongside chunk. Error: 404 triggers ErrorBoundary.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would keep useEffect fetch - waterfall persists.

title: Add accessible 3D scene with keyboard navigation and screen reader announcements
business_context: 3D viewer inaccessible to keyboard/SR users. Need keyboard-driven camera + ARIA announcements of focused object.
reasoning: Maintain list of focusable objects with metadata. Tab cycles through; ARIA live region announces. Camera moves to focused object. Reduced motion uses snap instead of lerp.

after_state.description: Tab focus + ARIA live region + camera-to-focus animation.

files.purpose: Keyboard navigation + ARIA live region + reduced-motion respect

testing_strategy: Keyboard test: Tab cycles focus and announces label. SR test: NVDA reads announcement. Reduced motion: snap rather than lerp.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would ignore a11y entirely. Adding aria-live + keyboard is a clear win.

title: Add tenant-scoped quotas for storage and compute with grace period
business_context: Some tenants exceed allocated storage. Need quota enforcement with grace period and reminders before hard cutoff.

files.content: export const upload = async (req: any, res: any) => { /* no check */ res.json({ ok: true }); };

reasoning: Org has plan with quotas. Nightly job sums usage per tenant. Soft warning state, then graceUntil, then hard block. Email notifications on transitions.

after_state.description: Quota model + nightly recalc + grace period + warnings.

files.purpose: Per-org quota state
Nightly recalc with grace + state transitions and email
Middleware blocks writes when blocked state

migration_strategy: Step 1: Seed Quota for each org
Step 3: Apply middleware to write endpoints
testing_strategy: Recalc test: usage at 85%, state -> warning. Grace expiry: at 101% past graceUntil, state -> blocked. Block: middleware returns 402 on write.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would block immediately at 100% - no grace, alienates customers. Soft transitions + email give time to react.
